problem: Why does acute pain become chronic in some people — what neural change occurs?
mechanism: Small molecule inhibitor of enzyme active site
hypothesis: Why does acute pain become chronic in some people — what neural change occurs? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Small. Small molecule inhibitor of enzyme active site directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Retrospective cohort analysis with 13 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The art of science is asking the right question, not answering. — Max Planck paraphrase
